articles.title: AFTERNOON WITH CRUSOE CLOUD
articles.summary: Fine-tuning shouldn't require a platform build. Crusoe Serverless Fine-Tuning is now in private preview — submit a job, get your weights back, ship your model. No cluster provisioning. No surprise bills. No infrastructure tax.

articles.title: Request early access to Crusoe Serverless Fine-Tuning
THE NEW STARTUP BOTTLENECK
articles.summary: Companies are shipping so much that they aren't learning anything. They are able to build a lot more, but learning hasn't caught up. Learning capacity has become a bottleneck. Shipping too fast can become expensive as a habit. The discipline to learn has to come from the founder.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN OFFERS $2M API CREDITS TO EVERY YC STARTUP FOR EQUITY
articles.summary: Y Combinator's Winter 2026 batch has 199 companies. Most are building AI tools. OpenAI's offer of $2 million API credits for every YC startup potentially totals $400 million in credits. The company is asking for 2% equity per startup at $100 million valuations. There is a risk that OpenAI may study and copy ideas.

articles.title: WHAT GAME CAN YOUNG FOUNDERS STILL WIN?
articles.summary: Primitive Crypto's Dovey Wan reports a real founder identity crisis in SF and Shenzhen, with multiple founders returning capital to join US LLM labs or Chinese robotics companies because the opportunity cost feels higher than running their own startup. Hyper-industrialized AI in biotech, robotics, aerospace, defense, and energy is capital-dense, regulation-heavy, and sovereign-guarded, ruling out the college-dropout path, while a fintech-2010s analog plays out as operators buy legacy ops and rewire them with AI for hybrid operators rather than default tech founders.

articles.title: SCHMOOZING IS DEAD, AGENTS ARE HITTING 120% OF HUMANS, GROWTH IS THE ONLY THING THAT MATTERS
articles.summary: Marc Benioff went on 20VC and said something Salesforce literally cannot deliver. He wishes every customer could fully deploy his software before signing the contract. The CEO of the biggest B2B SaaS company in history is publicly conceding that the agentic era broke his core motion. The product has to work on day 30, not year 3, and a Series A now goes to founders growing $2M to $500M like Replit, not the $10M to $20M that used to be a slam dunk.

articles.title: RENT THE INTELLIGENCE. OWN THE CONTEXT
articles.summary: Token control matters, but the bigger enterprise risk is letting the same vendor own the context layer. Enterprises can rent intelligence from whoever is best, but they should own the memory layer that makes intelligence useful. Models and agents will converge, but a company's working memory will not because it is made of promises, exceptions, scars, and decisions.

articles.title: YOU CLOSED 3 DEALS THIS QUARTER WITHOUT OUTBOUND. HOW MANY COULD YOU CLOSE WITH IT?
articles.summary: Lightfield [10] is the AI-native CRM with outbound agents. You talk to customers while they score accounts against your won deals, draft sequences from your conversations, and surface warm intro paths. Agents build the list and run the sequences. You sell.

articles.title: SOCLEADS 3.0
articles.summary: Extract emails and leads from social platforms and Google Maps using location-based scraping filters.

articles.title: LOBEHUB
articles.summary: Coordinate and manage teams of AI agents that collaborate on tasks across models and communication channels.

articles.title: LANDINGHERO AI
articles.summary: Turn your website into a 24/7 AI salesperson that answers visitor questions with voice conversations and captures leads.

articles.title: NEED SERIES C? CALL A16Z
articles.summary: Personal Injury succeeded as a business category by finding an economical way of making a value-aligned results-based service appeal to lots of people. The industry discovered unit economics that made a service that was previously out of scope for most people possible at scale. AI is in a similar position: it is an accelerator or enabler of new consumer business models.

articles.title: WHAT DO YOUR STARTUP ADVISORS SAY ABOUT YOU?
articles.summary: Advisors should fill genuine gaps in knowledge and connections, not be used as workarounds for needed hires. Real relationships, not formal agreements with strangers, provide meaningful advisory benefits. Engaging advisors who understand and support the business without requiring frequent meetings adds real value.

articles.title: DEEL'S ACCELERATE OR DIE MOMENT
articles.summary: Deel is growing 50% at $1.5B ARR with 17% EBITDA margins. Nobody public except Palantir is matching that pace. The CFO calls 2026 "accelerate or die" and Deel is treating it like a literal deadline. They opened a "Ghostbuster" role with a $10M waste-cutting target, shipped four major AI launches in three weeks, bought Sastrify, and the underlying story is that this playbook only works for companies sitting on a real moat to reinvest into, which Deel quietly built in payroll a decade ago.

articles.title: HOW TO WRITE A FORWARDABLE EMAIL
articles.summary: The person you're asking to forward your intro should be able to hit F in Superhuman, type one line on top, and send it.

articles.title: FINITE AND INFINITE MINDSETS
articles.summary: Win the game in front of you, but build like the game never ends.
